+1 on the suggestion. If you are working on this, a few comments: 

  - applicationType should also be part of ApplicationSubmissionContext
  - command-line tool to list applications (bin/yarn tool) should support filtering based on type
  - type should be a string
MAPREDUCE-4346 has some ideas on how this could be used.

Attaching patch.

Thanks,
Mayank

Looked at the patch. Comments follow:

 - ApplicationSubmissionContext: Rename getAppType to getApplicationType, it's better to be verbose and clear in the API. Similarly the setter.
 - Similarly in ApplicationReport, for both the records in yarn_protos.proto, RMApp, and RMAppImpl.

 - I think the application-type is optional from the submission side - so
    -- Can you add two utils for creating ApplicationSubmissionContext, one which takes in app-type and one which doesn't. Surprised we don't have a util for ApplicationSubmissionContext
    -- Please add a test using MockRM to verify that if the client provides a type that is respected and if a client doesn't, the default "YARN" is picked up.

 - We should have a limit on the length of this string, I'd start with 15 or 20. If client provides a longer string, we should just truncate it.
 - Please add a test for the above limit.

Please file a mapreduce ticket for tracking MR changes. That can go in separately.

Thanks Vinod for the review.

Incorporated all your comments. Created MAPREDUCE-5246 for the mapred changes.

Attaching both the patches.

Thanks,
Mayank

This looks much better. A few nits to fix:

YarnConfiguration: DEFAULT_APPLICATION_TYPE_LENGTH should simply be called APPLICATION_TYPE_LENGTH?

MockRM
 - *All* the changes in MockRM lack standard formatting
 - It can use the default app-type constant instead of hard-coding it.
 - Instead of changing the root-level constructor, you can add a new one that takes the default app-type

Once you do the above, TestRMRestart changes shouldn't really be needed.
Filtering based on type should be available both from the CLI as well as via the webservices.

Thanks Vinod for review. 

I have updated all your comments.

[~hitesh]

Application type is part of the application report and we are not doing any filtering neither in CLI nor in rest api.
I think user can get the application report and filter at their end thats what seems to work till now for other attributes as well.

If we want attribute based filtering then probably we need to add more apis.
If you think thats useful I can create a seprate JIRA and will work on that.

Thoughts?

Thanks,
Mayank

I would say that listing applications based on type ( via CLI or webservices ) should become a feature of the framework. Need not be part of this jira but it should be part of the framework. Filtering 10000 apps for a particular type is not good usability in my opinion.

MAPREDUCE-5246 tracks MR changes, so need to put them here. Even so, changes in NotRunningJob and TestClientServiceDelegate are unnecessary.

More comments on YARN changes, some of it I forgot before, apologies.
 - BuilderUtils apis: The one with no appType can call the one with appType passing appType as null. No need to duplicate all the code.
 - MockRM: No need for a appType field. Just make the constructor which doesn't take in a appType to call the one which takes in appType. But set appType to null so that ClientRMService does the replacement with default name - no need for duplicating that code here.

Hitesh, can you please file a separate ticket for your proposal? Tx.

I agree with Hitesh, I think this should be in the web UI and webservices as well as the CLI.   This could be very useful to anyone debugging their application that uses the web UI, SE looking for patterns or issues with particular type of application, tools using the webservices to aggregate info and create their own useful experiences, etc. 

Mayank, I'm not sure what you consider attributes?  Are you referring just to the filtering part?  The web ui and webservices print almost everything that is a part of the application report.  

I'm OK with web ui/webservices being added under a separate jira but would have rather seen them done here with the CLI part.

[~tgraves][~hitesh]

I am 100% in agreement with you guys that we need to have filtering and thats very important.
What I was trying to say (may be not clear at the first attempt) that we need new apis/interface which can do filtering based on parameters and return the results required. lets say I want application based on status/type/user/application name etc etc.

We did not do the filtering neither in CLI or web apps in scope of this JIRA. I will shortly file the seprate JIRA and work on that for CLI and web apps.

[~vinodkv]
Thanks for the review and will update the latest patch shortly.

Thanks,
Mayank

Sorry if I wasn't clear. I think we might be mixing terms here. In my mind there is showing them at all in the web ui/webservices and then there is the additional thing of supporting filtering on them.  I agree with you that the filtering part is separate. The showing them in web ui and webservices to me is the same thing as showing them in the output of the yarn application CLI command.

Updating the patch with vinod's comments.

Thanks,
Mayank

bq. The showing them in web ui and webservices to me is the same thing as showing them in the output of the yarn application CLI command. 
+1

Is there a contract defined on how the app type will be used? i.e. will it be a strict string match or lower case comparison? It would be good to define it in the documentation so that eventually when a history server is built out and starts using some pluggable modules to display history based on app type, the usage is clear.

bq.  It seems like the framework is implicitly chopping off the type based on a max length. Does it make sense to throw an error instead of allowing such submissions. Please refer to my previous comment on defining a contract of how the type is meant to be used - we should also define how it is meant to be set too.
I think chopping off is better than throwing exceptions. We will eventually have all sorts of limits on various fields where truncating is a better choice.

We should make the limit itself part of the javadoc for ApplicationSubmissionContext.setApplicationType() explicitly.

Sorry for the confusion. Thanks Thoms, Vinod for clearing it :)

Thanks,
Mayank

Oh, so close.
 - As seems to be the consensus, apps web-service doesn't have filtering based on appType. Please file a ticket.
 - I wasn't clear, apologies. Fundamentally, no-one needs to explicitly set the default appType. Either they set the name they desire or set nothing(null). So, MockRM.submitApp() doesn't need the null-checks & filling default-name. It can just set the passed in Type.
 - We don't need BuilderUtils.newApplicationReport that doesn't take appType. We only need that for ApplicationSubmissionContext as users only use this for creating a record, not application-report.
 - AppBlock: Type -> Application Type
 - Similarly AppsBlock
 - TestRMWebServicesApps: type -> applicationType

Unrelated to your patch: We need the html/JS escaping even in AppBlock. Please file a ticket.

comments.body:
+1 on the suggestion. If you are working on this, a few comments: 

  - applicationType should also be part of ApplicationSubmissionContext
  - command-line tool to list applications (bin/yarn tool) should support filtering based on type
  - type should be a string
MAPREDUCE-4346 has some ideas on how this could be used.

Attaching patch.

Thanks,
Mayank
